content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of all my findings. Let me organize everything systematically.

## Comprehensive Rearden Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Rearden project codebase. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. CANDIDATE PROFILE PAGE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\pages\CandidateProfile\CandidateProfile.tsx`

**Layout**:
- Two-column grid layout (video + info)
- Left column: VideoPlayer component (sticky on desktop, static on mobile)
- Right column: Candidate info sections
- Responsive: 2-column on desktop → 1-column on tablet/mobile

**Data Displayed**:
- Name (teal, large heading)
- Title (secondary text)
- Meta info: Location, Experience (years), Availability status
- About section: Bio text
- Skills section: SkillTag components (first 4 skills displayed, flex wrap)
- Action buttons: "Contact Candidate" (primary), "Back to Search" (ghost)

**Data Fetching**:
- Uses `useApi<Candidate>('/candidates/{id}')` hook
- Auto-fetches on component mount with route param `{id}`
- Shows loading spinner during fetch
- Shows error state if candidate not found

**Styling**: `CandidateProfile.module.scss` with:
- `$space*` variables for spacing
- `$accentTeal`, `$accentPurple` for colors
- Grid layout with gap, container mixin, responsive mixins
- `.profile` (main grid), `.videoSection` (sticky positioning), `.info` (flex column)
- `.nameSection`, `.meta`, `.skills`, `.actions`, `.section` sub-classes
- Loading and error state styles

---

### 2. REGISTER PAGE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\pages\Register\Register.tsx`

**Form Fields**:
1. Full Name (text input)
2. Email (email input)
3. Job Title (text input)
4. Location (text input)
5. Years of Experience (number input)
6. Skills (text input, comma-separated, parsed into array)
7. Bio (textarea, 4 rows)
8. Video Introduction (VideoUpload component)

**Form Submission**:
- POST to `/api/candidates`
- Sends: `name`, `email`, `title`, `location`, `bio`, `skills[]`, `experience` (int), `videoUrl`, `availability` (hardcoded to "immediate")
- Response: `Candidate` object with auto-generated ID
- Redirects to `/candidate/{id}` on success

**Components Used**:
- Input component (with optional label, placeholder, type, onChange)
- Button component (primary variant, lg size, disabled when name or email empty)
- VideoUpload component with upload tracking

**Styling**: `Register.module.scss` with:
- `.register` (max-width 720px, container)
- `.form` (white background, border, padding, flex column)
- `.formGrid` (2-column grid on desktop → 1-column on mobile)
- `.bioField`, `.videoSection`, `.label`, `.textarea`, `.actions`

---

### 3. TYPES & DATA MODELS

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\packages\types\src\`

**Candidate Type**:
```typescript
interface Candidate extends User {
  role: "candidate";
  resume: string | null;
  skills: string[];
  experience: number;
  videoUrl: string | null;
  thumbnailUrl: string | null;
  location: string;
  title: string;
  bio: string;
  availability: "immediate" | "2weeks" | "1month" | "3months";
}
```

**User Base Type**:
```typescript
interface User {
  id: string;
  email: string;
  name: string;
  role: "candidate" | "recruiter";
  createdAt: string;
  updatedAt: string;
}
```

**Recruiter Type**:
```typescript
interface Recruiter extends User {
  role: "recruiter";
  company: string;
  position: string;
}
```

**ApiResponse Type**:
```typescript
interface ApiResponse<T = unknown> {
  success: boolean;
  data?: T;
  error?: string;
  meta?: Record<string, unknown>;
}
```

**Search Types**:
- `SearchQuery`: `{ query: string; filters?: SearchFilters }`
- `SearchFilters`: `{ skills?, location?, experienceMin?, experienceMax?, availability? }`
- `SearchResult`: `{ candidateId, score, matchReason }`
- `SearchResponse`: `{ results[], query, totalCandidates, searchTimeMs }`

**Chat Types**:
- `ChatMessage`: `{ id, conversationId, senderId, senderRole, text, createdAt }`
- `ChatConversation`: `{ id, candidateId, candidateName, candidateAvatar, isPinned, unreadCount, lastMessage, createdAt, updatedAt }`
- `ChatTab`: "all" | "unread" | "pinned"
- `WSClientEvent` / `WSServerEvent`: Various WebSocket event types

---

### 4. API ROUTES STRUCTURE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\src\app.ts`

**Routes**:
- `POST /api/candidates` - Create new candidate (in-memory, no database)
- `GET /api/candidates` - Get all candidates
- `GET /api/candidates/:id` - Get candidate by ID
- `POST /api/search` - Search candidates with scoring
- `POST /api/upload` - Upload file (stores in `/uploads` directory)
- `GET /api/upload/:filename` - Serve uploaded file
- `POST /api/chat` - Chat route handlers
- `GET /ws` - WebSocket connection for chat

**Database**: Currently using in-memory storage with `mockCandidates` array (no Prisma queries being used yet)

---

### 5. CANDIDATES API ROUTE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\src\routes\candidates.ts`

**GET /api/candidates**:
- Returns all candidates from in-memory store
- Response: `ApiResponse<Candidate[]>`

**GET /api/candidates/:id**:
- Finds candidate by ID
- Returns 404 if not found
- Response: `ApiResponse<Candidate>`

**POST /api/candidates**:
- Creates new candidate from request body
- Auto-generates: `id` (UUID), `createdAt`, `updatedAt` (ISO strings)
- Defaults: `resume: null`, `skills: []`, `experience: 0`, `videoUrl: null`, `thumbnailUrl: null`, etc.
- Merges provided fields with defaults (spread operator)
- Returns created candidate with 201 status

---

### 6. SEARCH API ROUTE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\src\routes\search.ts`

**POST /api/search**:
- Accepts `SearchQuery`: `{ query: string, filters?: SearchFilters }`
- Uses `searchCandidates()` function from `../lib/searchEngine.js`
- Includes 500ms artificial delay to simulate processing
- Returns `SearchResult[]` with `candidate` object attached
- Response format: `{ results, query, totalCandidates, searchTimeMs }`
- Tracks search execution time

---

### 7. UPLOAD API ROUTE

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\src\routes\upload.ts`

**POST /api/upload**:
- Accepts FormData with `file` field
- Validates: File must be provided and be a File instance
- Stores in `process.cwd()/uploads/` directory
- Generates filename: `{timestamp}-{uuid}{extension}`
- Returns: `{ url: "/api/upload/{filename}", filename }`

**GET /api/upload/:filename**:
- Serves uploaded file
- Sets correct Content-Type based on extension
- Supports: PDF, images (jpg/png/gif/webp), videos (mp4/webm), docs (doc/docx), txt
- Returns 404 if file not found

---

### 8. PRISMA SETUP

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\prisma\schema.prisma`

**Current Schema**:
```prisma
datasource db {
  provider = "sqlite"
}

generator client {
  provider = "prisma-client"
  output   = "../src/generated/prisma"
}

model User {
  id        String   @id @default(cuid())
  email     String   @unique
  name      String
  role      String   @default("candidate")
  createdAt DateTime @default(now())
  updatedAt DateTime @updatedAt
}
```

**Config**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\prisma.config.ts`
- Uses `defineConfig()` from `prisma/config`
- Database URL from `DATABASE_URL` environment variable
- Schema path: `prisma/schema.prisma`
- Migrations path: `prisma/migrations`

---

### 9. VIDEO COMPONENTS

**VideoPlayer.tsx**:
- Props: `{ src: string; poster?: string }`
- Features: Play/pause toggle, custom play button overlay, HTML5 video controls when playing
- Uses ref to control video element
- Styles: `VideoPlayer.module.scss` with `.wrapper`, `.video`, `.playButton`, `.icon`

**VideoUpload.tsx**:
- Props: `{ onUpload, uploading?, progress?, previewUrl? }`
- Features: Drag & drop zone, click to browse, file validation (video/* only)
- Shows upload progress bar with percentage
- Preview mode shows VideoPlayer with "Re-upload" button
- Drag state styling with `.dragging` class
- States: dropzone → uploading → preview

---

### 10. REUSABLE UI COMPONENTS

**Located**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\components/`

| Component | File | Props | Features |
|-----------|------|-------|----------|
| Button | Button.tsx | `variant` (primary/secondary/ghost), `size` (sm/md/lg), `onClick`, `type`, `href`, `disabled`, `children` | Motion animations, sound effects, link support |
| Input | Input.tsx | `label`, `error`, `icon`, `placeholder`, `value`, `onChange`, `type`, `name`, `id` | Wrapper layout, icon support, error messages |
| Select | Select.tsx | (Not read - in glob list) | - |
| SkillTag | SkillTag.tsx | `skill` (string), `variant` (default/match), `size` (sm/md) | Tag styling with variants |
| ScoreBadge | ScoreBadge.tsx | `score` (number) | Color variants: high (80+), medium (60-79), low (<60) |
| CandidateCard | CandidateCard.tsx | `candidate`, `score?`, `matchReason?`, `onClick?` | Thumbnail, name, title, location, skills preview, score badge |
| SearchBar | SearchBar.tsx | `value`, `onChange`, `loading` | (In glob, not fully read) |
| VideoPlayer | VideoPlayer.tsx | `src`, `poster?` | Custom play button, controls on play |
| VideoUpload | VideoUpload.tsx | `onUpload`, `uploading?`, `progress?`, `previewUrl?` | Drag & drop, progress tracking, preview |
| ChatInput | ChatInput.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| ChatMessage | ChatMessage.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| ConversationList | ConversationList.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| ChatPanel | ChatPanel.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Navbar | Navbar.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Sidebar | Sidebar.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| NotificationItem | NotificationItem.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| NotificationPanel | NotificationPanel.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Logo | Logo.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Layout | Layout.tsx | (Not fully read) | Main layout wrapper |
| PageTransition | PageTransition.tsx | (Not fully read) | Animation wrapper |

---

### 11. HOOKS & UTILITIES

**useApi.ts**:
- `useApi<T>(path: string)` → `{ data, loading, error, refetch }`
- Auto-fetches on mount
- Parses API response expecting `{ success, data: T }`

**useSearch.ts**:
- `useSearch()` → `{ query, setQuery, results, loading, error }`
- Debounced search: 400ms delay on query change
- Fetches `/candidates` if query empty (returns all with default scores)
- Fetches `/search` POST if query provided
- Returns `SearchResultWithCandidate[]` with attached candidate object

**useVideoUpload.ts**:
- `useVideoUpload()` → `{ upload, uploading, progress, previewUrl }`
- `upload(file)`: Returns Promise<string> (URL of uploaded file)
- Creates object URL for preview immediately
- Uses XMLHttpRequest with progress tracking
- POST to `/api/upload` with FormData

**useSound.ts**: (In glob, not read)

**useWebSocket.ts**: (In glob, not read)

**api.ts**:
- `apiFetch<T>(path, options?)`: Promise<T>
- Prepends `/api` to path
- Defaults `Content-Type: application/json`
- Throws if response not ok

---

### 12. ROUTER SETUP

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\router.tsx`

**Routes** (all under Layout wrapper):
- `/` → Landing
- `/search` → Search (candidate discovery)
- `/candidate/:id` → CandidateProfile
- `/register` → Register (candidate signup)
- `/chat` → Chat (main)
- `/chat/:conversationId` → Chat (specific conversation)

---

### 13. MOCK DATA

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\api\src\data\mockCandidates.ts`

**12 Sample Candidates** with full Candidate objects:
1. Sarah Chen - Senior Full Stack Engineer (React, TS, Node, GraphQL, PostgreSQL, 6yrs)
2. Marcus Johnson - Lead Backend Engineer (Python, Django, AWS, Docker, Redis, 8yrs)
3. Emily Rodriguez - Frontend Developer (Vue, JS, Tailwind, Figma, 4yrs)
4. Alex Kim - Mobile Engineer (React Native, Swift, Kotlin, Firebase, 5yrs)
5. Priya Patel - Senior Backend Developer (Java, Spring Boot, K8s, MongoDB, 7yrs)
6. Jordan Taylor - Staff Systems Engineer (Go, Rust, gRPC, Kafka, 9yrs)
7. Lisa Anderson - Frontend Engineer (Angular, RxJS, TS, Jest, Cypress, 3yrs)
8. David Nguyen - Full Stack Developer (React, Next.js, Vercel, Tailwind, Prisma, 5yrs)
9. Rachel Brooks - ML Engineer (Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Pandas, 6yrs)
10. Kevin Martinez - Principal Engineer (C#, .NET, Azure, SQL Server, 10yrs)
11. Nina Williams - Junior Full Stack Developer (Svelte, SvelteKit, TS, Supabase, 2yrs)
12. Carlos Santos - Senior Web Developer (PHP, Laravel, MySQL, Redis, Vue, 7yrs)

Each has: videos (Pexels), thumbnails, bio, availability, experience, location, title, skills array.

---

### 14. STYLING ARCHITECTURE

**SCSS Modules** with:
- `variables.scss`: Colors (`$accentTeal`, `$accentPurple`, `$textSecondary`, `$glassBorder`, etc.), spacing (`$space1-20`), typography (`$fontSize12-32`), shadows, borders, animations
- `mixins.scss`: Breakpoints (`@include tablet`, `@include mobile`), container layout, grid helpers
- All classes use camelCase
- Motion animations: Spring physics, transitions

---

### Summary Table of Key Files

files[12]{path,description}
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/web/src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx,Profile page: 2-col grid, video+info, motion animations
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/web/src/pages/Register/Register.tsx,Form with 8 fields (name email title location experience skills bio video) POST to /api/candidates
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/packages/types/src/index.ts,Export barrel: Candidate User Recruiter ApiResponse Search Chat types
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/packages/types/src/candidate.ts,Extends User with video skills experience availability location title bio resume
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/api/src/routes/candidates.ts,GET /api/candidates GET /:id POST / (create with UUID auto-generation)
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/api/src/routes/search.ts,POST /api/search with scoring via searchCandidates() lib function
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts,POST /api/upload file storage in /uploads GET /:filename serve with correct Content-Type
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/web/src/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.tsx,Custom play button overlay HTML5 video with poster
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/web/src/components/VideoUpload/VideoUpload.tsx,Drag-drop zone file input progress bar preview mode XMLHttpRequest upload
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/web/src/router.tsx,6 routes: / /search /candidate/:id /register /chat /chat/:conversationId
C:/Users/yakik/Documents/projects/rearden/apps/api/src/app.ts,Hono app with CORS logger routes candidates search upload chat WebSocket

---

toolUseResult.prompt:
Explore the Rearden project codebase to understand the current candidate profile system, content creation, and data models. I need to understand:

1. **CandidateProfile page**: Read `apps/web/src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx` and its SCSS module fully. Understand the current layout (video + info grid), what data it displays, and how it fetches data.

2. **Register page**: Read `apps/web/src/pages/Register/Register.tsx` — understand the current form fields and how candidates are created.

3. **Types**: Read `packages/types/src/index.ts` — understand the `Candidate` type and any related types. What fields exist?

4. **API routes**: Explore `apps/api/src/` — find the candidates routes, how they're structured, what endpoints exist. Read the Prisma schema if it exists.

5. **VideoPlayer and VideoUpload components**: Read `apps/web/src/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.tsx` and `apps/web/src/components/VideoUpload/VideoUpload.tsx` — understand the current video handling.

6. **Router setup**: Read `apps/web/src/App.tsx` or wherever routes are defined — understand the route structure.

7. **Existing UI components**: List all components in `apps/web/src/components/` — I need to know what reusable UI exists (Button, Input, SkillTag, etc.)

Report all findings comprehensively — file paths, types, component props, API endpoints, database schema.

content.text:
Perfect! Now let me compile a comprehensive report of all my findings. Let me organize everything systematically.

## Comprehensive Rearden Codebase Exploration Report

I've thoroughly explored the Rearden project codebase. Here's what I found:

---

### 1. CANDIDATE PROFILE PAGE

- Shows loading spinner during fetch
- Shows error state if candidate not found

**Styling**: `CandidateProfile.module.scss` with:

- `$accentTeal`, `$accentPurple` for colors
- Grid layout with gap, container mixin, responsive mixins

```prisma
datasource db {

generator client {
```

### 9. VIDEO COMPONENTS

**VideoPlayer.tsx**:
- Props: `{ src: string; poster?: string }`
- Features: Play/pause toggle, custom play button overlay, HTML5 video controls when playing
- Uses ref to control video element
- Styles: `VideoPlayer.module.scss` with `.wrapper`, `.video`, `.playButton`, `.icon`

- Preview mode shows VideoPlayer with "Re-upload" button
- Drag state styling with `.dragging` class

---

### 10. REUSABLE UI COMPONENTS

**Located**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\components/`

| Component | File | Props | Features |
|-----------|------|-------|----------|
| Button | Button.tsx | `variant` (primary/secondary/ghost), `size` (sm/md/lg), `onClick`, `type`, `href`, `disabled`, `children` | Motion animations, sound effects, link support |
| Input | Input.tsx | `label`, `error`, `icon`, `placeholder`, `value`, `onChange`, `type`, `name`, `id` | Wrapper layout, icon support, error messages |
| Select | Select.tsx | (Not read - in glob list) | - |

| ScoreBadge | ScoreBadge.tsx | `score` (number) | Color variants: high (80+), medium (60-79), low (<60) |
| CandidateCard | CandidateCard.tsx | `candidate`, `score?`, `matchReason?`, `onClick?` | Thumbnail, name, title, location, skills preview, score badge |
| SearchBar | SearchBar.tsx | `value`, `onChange`, `loading` | (In glob, not fully read) |

| NotificationPanel | NotificationPanel.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Logo | Logo.tsx | (Not fully read) | - |
| Layout | Layout.tsx | (Not fully read) | Main layout wrapper |
| PageTransition | PageTransition.tsx | (Not fully read) | Animation wrapper |

---

### 11. HOOKS & UTILITIES

**useApi.ts**:
- `useApi<T>(path: string)` → `{ data, loading, error, refetch }`
- Auto-fetches on mount
- Parses API response expecting `{ success, data: T }`

**useSearch.ts**:
- `useSearch()` → `{ query, setQuery, results, loading, error }`
- Debounced search: 400ms delay on query change
- Fetches `/candidates` if query empty (returns all with default scores)
- Fetches `/search` POST if query provided
- Returns `SearchResultWithCandidate[]` with attached candidate object

**useVideoUpload.ts**:
- `useVideoUpload()` → `{ upload, uploading, progress, previewUrl }`
- `upload(file)`: Returns Promise<string> (URL of uploaded file)
- Creates object URL for preview immediately
- Uses XMLHttpRequest with progress tracking
- POST to `/api/upload` with FormData

**useSound.ts**: (In glob, not read)

**useWebSocket.ts**: (In glob, not read)

**api.ts**:
- `apiFetch<T>(path, options?)`: Promise<T>
- Prepends `/api` to path
- Defaults `Content-Type: application/json`
- Throws if response not ok

---

### 12. ROUTER SETUP

**File**: `C:\Users\yakik\Documents\projects\rearden\apps\web\src\router.tsx`

**Routes** (all under Layout wrapper):
- `/` → Landing
- `/search` → Search (candidate discovery)
- `/candidate/:id` → CandidateProfile
- `/register` → Register (candidate signup)
- `/chat` → Chat (main)

### 13. MOCK DATA

9. Rachel Brooks - ML Engineer (Python, TensorFlow, PyTorch, Pandas, 6yrs)
10. Kevin Martinez - Principal Engineer (C#, .NET, Azure, SQL Server, 10yrs)
11. Nina Williams - Junior Full Stack Developer (Svelte, SvelteKit, TS, Supabase, 2yrs)
12. Carlos Santos - Senior Web Developer (PHP, Laravel, MySQL, Redis, Vue, 7yrs)

Each has: videos (Pexels), thumbnails, bio, availability, experience, location, title, skills array.

---

### 14. STYLING ARCHITECTURE

**SCSS Modules** with:
- `variables.scss`: Colors (`$accentTeal`, `$accentPurple`, `$textSecondary`, `$glassBorder`, etc.), spacing (`$space1-20`), typography (`$fontSize12-32`), shadows, borders, animations
- `mixins.scss`: Breakpoints (`@include tablet`, `@include mobile`), container layout, grid helpers
- All classes use camelCase
- Motion animations: Spring physics, transitions

---

### Summary Table of Key Files

message.content:
Your task is to create a detailed summary of the conversation so far, paying close attention to the user's explicit requests and your previous actions.
This summary should be thorough in capturing technical details, code patterns, and architectural decisions that would be essential for continuing development work without losing context.

Before providing your final summary, wrap your analysis in <analysis> tags to organize your thoughts and ensure you've covered all necessary points. In your analysis process:

1. Chronologically analyze each message and section of the conversation. For each section thoroughly identify:
   - The user's explicit requests and intents
   - Your approach to addressing the user's requests
   - Key decisions, technical concepts and code patterns
   - Specific details like:
     - file names
     - full code snippets
     - function signatures
     - file edits
  - Errors that you ran into and how you fixed them
  - Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
2. Double-check for technical accuracy and completeness, addressing each required element thoroughly.

Your summary should include the following sections:

1. Primary Request and Intent: Capture all of the user's explicit requests and intents in detail
2. Key Technical Concepts: List all important technical concepts, technologies, and frameworks discussed.
3. Files and Code Sections: Enumerate specific files and code sections examined, modified, or created. Pay special attention to the most recent messages and include full code snippets where applicable and include a summary of why this file read or edit is important.
4. Errors and fixes: List all errors that you ran into, and how you fixed them. Pay special attention to specific user feedback that you received, especially if the user told you to do something differently.
5. Problem Solving: Document problems solved and any ongoing troubleshooting efforts.
6. All user messages: List ALL user messages that are not tool results. These are critical for understanding the users' feedback and changing intent.
6. Pending Tasks: Outline any pending tasks that you have explicitly been asked to work on.
7. Current Work: Describe in detail precisely what was being worked on immediately before this summary request, paying special attention to the most recent messages from both user and assistant. Include file names and code snippets where applicable.
8. Optional Next Step: List the next step that you will take that is related to the most recent work you were doing. IMPORTANT: ensure that this step is DIRECTLY in line with the user's most recent explicit requests, and the task you were working on immediately before this summary request. If your last task was concluded, then only list next steps if they are explicitly in line with the users request. Do not start on tangential requests or really old requests that were already completed without confirming with the user first.
                       If there is a next step, include direct quotes from the most recent conversation showing exactly what task you were working on and where you left off. This should be verbatim to ensure there's no drift in task interpretation.

Here's an example of how your output should be structured:

<example>
<analysis>
[Your thought process, ensuring all points are covered thoroughly and accurately]
</analysis>

<summary>
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   [Detailed description]

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - [Concept 1]
   - [Concept 2]
   - [...]

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - [File Name 1]
      - [Summary of why this file is important]
      - [Summary of the changes made to this file, if any]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [File Name 2]
      - [Important Code Snippet]
   - [...]

4. Errors and fixes:
    - [Detailed description of error 1]:
      - [How you fixed the error]
      - [User feedback on the error if any]
    - [...]

5. Problem Solving:
   [Description of solved problems and ongoing troubleshooting]

6. All user messages: 
    - [Detailed non tool use user message]
    - [...]

7. Pending Tasks:
   - [Task 1]
   - [Task 2]
   - [...]

8. Current Work:
   [Precise description of current work]

9. Optional Next Step:
   [Optional Next step to take]

</summary>
</example>

Please provide your summary based on the conversation so far, following this structure and ensuring precision and thoroughness in your response. 

There may be additional summarization instructions provided in the included context. If so, remember to follow these instructions when creating the above summary. Examples of instructions include:
<example>
## Compact Instructions
When summarizing the conversation focus on typescript code changes and also remember the mistakes you made and how you fixed them.
</example>

<example>
# Summary instructions
When you are using compact - please focus on test output and code changes. Include file reads verbatim.
</example>


IMPORTANT: Do NOT use any tools. You MUST respond with ONLY the <summary>...</summary> block as your text output.

content.text:
Let me chronologically analyze the conversation: 1. **Previous session context (from summary)**: A complete 6-phase "RemiliaNET-Style UI Redesign" was implemented - transforming the entire visual identity from glassmorphism to a retro-computing aesthetic. This covered ~41 files across design tokens, variables, mixins, animations, global styles, theme simplification, core components, content components, chat components, and page styles. All builds passed. 2. **This session starts**: The user asked to fix a PageTransition animation bug where navigating via client-side routing causes pages to get stuck at opacity:0. 3. **PageTransition fix attempt 1**: Changed `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` to `mode="popLayout"` in Layout.tsx. This caused duplicate DOM nodes and a large empty space. 4. **PageTransition fix - root cause found**: Discovered that ALL 4 page components (Landing, Search, Register, CandidateProfile) wrapped themselves in `` while Layout.tsx ALSO wrapped `` in `` - creating nested PageTransitions. Fix: reverted to `mode="wait"`, removed `` from all 4 page components. This worked correctly. 5. **Mobile QA testing**: User asked to check mobile version. Found and fixed 4 bugs: - Navbar overflow (reduced gaps, shrunk fonts, hid sound toggle on mobile) - Skill tags clipping (increased max-height from 24px to 56px) - Search page spacing (tighter padding/margins on mobile) - Landing page spacing (tighter padding across all sections on mobile) - Also fixed Register and CandidateProfile font sizes for mobile 6. **Page transition flash bug**: User reported a flash/blink when switching pages. Root cause: `AnimatePresence mode="wait"` always has a blank frame between exit and enter animations. First tried simplifying to 150ms opacity-only fade (no y offset, no spring). This still had a brief blank frame inherent to `mode="wait"`. Final fix: removed `AnimatePresence` and `PageTransition` entirely from Layout.tsx - instant page switches fitting the retro aesthetic. Pages still have their own internal entrance animations. 7. **Instagram-style profile redesign request**: User asked for a major profile redesign with: - Tabs: "Posts" | "Video" | "Vacancies" - Vertical video format - Posts with hashtags - Resume attachment with link in profile header → new page showing formatted resume text + download 8. **Plan mode entered**: Two Explore agents were launched to investigate the codebase. Both completed with comprehensive findings about the current architecture, types, API routes, components, hooks, contexts, and styling patterns. 9. **Current state**: In plan mode, Phase 1 (exploration) is complete. Need to proceed to Phase 2 (design) with a Plan agent, then write the plan file and exit plan mode. Key files modified in this session: - `Layout.tsx` - Multiple changes: mode="wait" → mode="popLayout" → back to mode="wait" → finally removed AnimatePresence entirely - `PageTransition.tsx` - Simplified from spring+y to 150ms opacity fade, but ultimately unused since Layout.tsx no longer uses it - `Landing.tsx`, `Search.tsx`, `Register.tsx`, `CandidateProfile.tsx` - Removed `` wrappers and imports - `Navbar.module.scss` - Mobile responsive fixes - `CandidateCard.module.scss` - Skill tags max-height fix - `Search.module.scss` - Mobile spacing fixes - `Landing.module.scss` - Mobile spacing fixes - `Register.module.scss` - Mobile spacing fixes - `CandidateProfile.module.scss` - Mobile font size fix Current state of key files after all changes: Layout.tsx: Simple outlet, no AnimatePresence ```tsx import { Outlet, useLocation } from "react-router-dom"; import { Navbar } from "@/components/Navbar/Navbar"; import { Sidebar } from "@/components/Sidebar/Sidebar"; import styles from "./Layout.module.scss"; export function Layout() { const location = useLocation(); const isChatPage = location.pathname.startsWith("/chat"); return (
{!isChatPage && }
); } ``` PageTransition.tsx (exists but unused by Layout): ```tsx import { motion } from "motion/react"; import type { ReactNode } from "react"; interface PageTransitionProps { children: ReactNode; } export function PageTransition({ children }: PageTransitionProps) { return ( {children} ); } ``` Exploration findings for the new feature: - Candidate type has `resume: string | null` field already - API has upload endpoint supporting PDF, images, videos, docs - Chat page has a tab pattern (ChatTabBar) that can be reused - useApi hook for data fetching - 12 mock candidates in memory - SCSS modules with camelCase classes, IBM Plex Mono font, RemiliaNET light theme 1. Primary Request and Intent: - **Session start**: Fix PageTransition animation bug (pages stuck at opacity:0 on client-side navigation) - **Mobile QA**: User asked "Просмотри мобильную версию и пройдись по ее багам тоже, там много ui косяков" (Review mobile version and fix UI bugs) - **Page transition flash**: User reported "При воспроизведении анимации переключения страниц происходит небольшой лаг, страница мигает, потом проходит анимация" (flash/blink when switching pages) - **Instagram-style profile redesign** (CURRENT/PENDING): User requested a major profile page overhaul with tabs ("посты" | "видео" | "вакансии"), vertical video format, posts with hashtags, and resume attachment with a dedicated formatted view page and download capability 2. Key Technical Concepts: - Motion v12 `AnimatePresence` modes: `wait` (sequential, causes blank frame), `popLayout` (overlap, causes duplication), removed entirely (instant, retro-authentic) - Nested `PageTransition` wrappers causing animation conflicts — Layout.tsx wrapped `` AND each page component wrapped itself - SCSS responsive design with `@include mobile` (480px) and `@include tablet` (768px) mixins - IBM Plex Mono monospace font — wider than standard fonts, requiring adjusted spacing on mobile - RemiliaNET retro-computing aesthetic: light theme (#EAEAEA bg), solid borders (#BBBBBB), 3D buttons, silver metallic headers - Turborepo monorepo: `apps/web` (Vite+React), `apps/api` (Hono), `packages/types` (raw .ts) - In-memory data store with 12 mock candidates (no Prisma queries active yet) - Existing `resume: string | null` field on Candidate type - API upload endpoint supports PDF, images, videos, docs with proper Content-Type serving - ChatTabBar pattern reusable for profile tabs (motion layoutId indicator, spring animations) 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`apps/web/src/components/Layout/Layout.tsx`** — Central layout wrapper. Multiple iterations: added `popLayout`, reverted to `wait`, finally removed `AnimatePresence` and `PageTransition` entirely for instant page switches. ```tsx import { Outlet, useLocation } from "react-router-dom"; import { Navbar } from "@/components/Navbar/Navbar"; import { Sidebar } from "@/components/Sidebar/Sidebar"; import styles from "./Layout.module.scss"; export function Layout() { const location = useLocation(); const isChatPage = location.pathname.startsWith("/chat"); return (
{!isChatPage && }
); } ``` - **`apps/web/src/components/PageTransition/PageTransition.tsx`** — Simplified from spring+y offset to 150ms opacity fade. Exists but no longer imported by Layout.tsx. ```tsx import { motion } from "motion/react"; import type { ReactNode } from "react"; interface PageTransitionProps { children: ReactNode; } export function PageTransition({ children }: PageTransitionProps) { return ( {children} ); } ``` - **`apps/web/src/pages/Landing/Landing.tsx`** — Removed `PageTransition` import and wrapper. Removed `` / `` tags around the landing div. - **`apps/web/src/pages/Search/Search.tsx`** — Removed `PageTransition` import and wrapper. Fixed extra `` that resulted from wrapper removal. - **`apps/web/src/pages/Register/Register.tsx`** — Removed `PageTransition` import and wrapper. - **`apps/web/src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.tsx`** — Removed `PageTransition` import and all 3 wrappers (loading, error, main states). Also added `@include mobile` font-size reduction for `.name`. - **`apps/web/src/components/Navbar/Navbar.module.scss`** — Mobile responsive fixes: - `.links`: Added `@include tablet { gap: $space4; }` and `@include mobile { gap: $space2; }` - `.link`: Added `@include mobile { font-size: $fontSize12; letter-spacing: 0; }` - `.soundToggle`: Added `@include mobile { display: none; }` to save space - **`apps/web/src/components/CandidateCard/CandidateCard.module.scss`** — Changed `.skills` `max-height` from `24px` to `56px` to allow 2 rows of tags with monospace font. - **`apps/web/src/pages/Search/Search.module.scss`** — Mobile spacing: added `@include mobile` blocks for `.search` (reduced padding), `.header` (reduced margin), `.title` (fontSize20), `.subtitle` (fontSize14), `.searchWrapper` (reduced margin). - **`apps/web/src/pages/Landing/Landing.module.scss`** — Mobile spacing: added `@include mobile` blocks for `.hero` (padding 32px), `.badge` (smaller margin/font), `.subheading` (14px, less margin), `.features` (32px padding), `.featureCard` (20px padding), `.stats` (24px padding), `.cta` (reduced padding), `.ctaHeading` (fontSize20). - **`apps/web/src/pages/Register/Register.module.scss`** — Added `@include mobile` for `.register` (reduced padding) and `.title` (fontSize20). - **`apps/web/src/pages/CandidateProfile/CandidateProfile.module.scss`** — Added `@include mobile` for `.name` (fontSize20). **Exploration findings for Instagram-style profile feature:** - **`packages/types/src/candidate.ts`** — Candidate type already has `resume: string | null` field. Full type: ```typescript interface Candidate extends User { role: "candidate"; resume: string | null; skills: string[]; experience: number; videoUrl: string | null; thumbnailUrl: string | null; location: string; title: string; bio: string; availability: "immediate" | "2weeks" | "1month" | "3months"; } ``` - **`apps/api/src/routes/upload.ts`** — Supports file upload (POST /api/upload) and serving (GET /api/upload/:filename). Handles PDF, images, videos, docs with correct Content-Type headers. - **`apps/web/src/pages/Chat/ChatTabBar.tsx`** — Reusable tab pattern with motion layoutId indicator, spring animations. Uses array of `{ value, label }` objects. - **`apps/web/src/hooks/useApi.ts`** — `useApi(path)` returns `{ data, loading, error, refetch }`. - **`apps/web/src/router.tsx`** — Current routes: `/`, `/search`, `/candidate/:id`, `/register`, `/chat`, `/chat/:conversationId`. - **`apps/api/src/routes/candidates.ts`** — GET/POST endpoints, in-memory storage, UUID auto-generation. - **`apps/api/src/data/mockCandidates.ts`** — 12 fully populated mock candidates. 4. Errors and Fixes: - **`AnimatePresence mode="popLayout"` caused duplicate DOM nodes**: Two copies of CandidateProfile appeared simultaneously with a large empty gap above content. Fixed by reverting to `mode="wait"` and removing nested PageTransition wrappers from all page components instead. - **Extra `` in Search.tsx after PageTransition removal**: When replacing `` with ``, an extra closing tag was created. Fixed by removing the duplicate ``. - **Page transition flash/blink with `mode="wait"`**: Even after simplifying to 150ms opacity-only fade, `mode="wait"` inherently has a blank frame between exit and enter. Fixed by removing `AnimatePresence` and `PageTransition` from Layout.tsx entirely — instant page switches. - **Playwright click timeouts on NavLink**: Clicks succeed but Playwright reports 5000ms timeout because NavLink re-renders (active class change) during click. This is a Playwright testing artifact, not a user-visible issue. Navigation confirmed working via `window.location.pathname` evaluation. 5. Problem Solving: - **PageTransition nested wrapper bug**: Diagnosed by examining DOM chain via `browser_evaluate`, found parent div with `opacity: 0`. Traced to dual PageTransition wrappers (Layout + page component). Systematic solution: single source of animation in Layout only. - **Mobile UI overhaul**: Used Playwright at 375x812 viewport to screenshot all 5 pages, identified spacing/overflow issues caused by monospace font being wider than the previous Inter font, and systematically added mobile breakpoints. - **Flash elimination**: Iterated through multiple approaches (popLayout → wait+simplified → removed entirely) to find the approach that completely eliminates visual artifacts. 6. All User Messages: - "Yes, fix pls" (in response to offer to fix PageTransition bug) - "Просмотри мобильную версию и пройдись по ее багам тоже, там много ui косяков." (Review mobile version and fix its UI bugs, there are many issues) - "При вопспроизведении анимации переключения страниц происходит небольшой лаг, страница мигает, потом проходит анимация. Посмотри через плейврайт попереключай страницы - увидишь" (When page transition animation plays there's a small lag, the page flashes, then the animation plays. Look through Playwright, switch pages - you'll see) - "Я хочу, чтобы профили с видосами были похожи на инстаграм, то есть будут вкладки "посты" | "видео" | "вакансии". Надо, чтобы формат видео был вертикальным. И можно было создавать посты с хэштегами. Еще надо, чтобы можно было прикрепить бумажное резюме - необходимо сделать на него ссылку в шапке профиля - при нажатии перекидывать на новую страницу, где будет показываться отформатированный текст из резюме и возможность его скачать." (I want profiles with videos to be like Instagram — tabs "posts" | "video" | "vacancies". Video format should be vertical. Should be able to create posts with hashtags. Also need to attach a paper resume — link in profile header — clicking opens a new page showing formatted resume text with download option) 7. Pending Tasks: - **Instagram-style profile redesign** — This is the active task. Currently in plan mode, Phase 1 (exploration) is complete with two Explore agents having returned comprehensive findings. Need to: 1. Launch a Plan agent (Phase 2: Design) 2. Write the plan to the plan file (Phase 4) 3. Exit plan mode for user approval (Phase 5) 8. Current Work: In **plan mode** for the Instagram-style profile redesign. Two Explore agents have completed Phase 1 with full codebase analysis covering: Candidate types, API routes (candidates, upload, search), all UI components, hooks (useApi, useSearch, useVideoUpload, useWebSocket, useSound), contexts (Chat, Notification, Sidebar, Theme, Sound), SCSS design system, router setup, mock data, ChatTabBar tab pattern, and Chat page mobile/desktop split pattern. The plan file at `C:\Users\yakik\.claude\plans\compressed-percolating-tower.md` still contains the old RemiliaNET redesign plan and needs to be overwritten with the new Instagram-style profile plan. 9. Optional Next Step: Continue plan mode workflow — launch a Plan agent to design the implementation for the Instagram-style profile feature based on the exploration findings, then write the final plan to the plan file and call ExitPlanMode. The user's request was: > "Я хочу, чтобы профили с видосами были похожи на инстаграм, то есть будут вкладки "посты" | "видео" | "вакансии". Надо, чтобы формат видео был вертикальным. И можно было создавать посты с хэштегами. Еще надо, чтобы можно было прикрепить бумажное резюме - необходимо сделать на него ссылку в шапке профиля - при нажатии перекидывать на новую страницу, где будет показываться отформатированный текст из резюме и возможность его скачать." The plan needs to cover: new types (Post, Video, Vacancy), API endpoints, profile page tabs UI, vertical video player, post creation with hashtags, resume upload/display/download page, updated registration flow, and mock data.
